title: mentha spicata subsp. tomentosa
intro: Mentha spicata subsp. tomentosa, commonly known as woolly mint, is a subspecies of the peppermint plant that is characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of woolly mint include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. Woolly mint is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation, improve mood, and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
